data.title: Asana Director Dustin A. Moskovitz Acquires 450,000 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameMoskovitz Dustin A.
RelationshipDirector, 10% Owner
TypePurchase
Amount$6,739,155
SEC FilingForm 4

Dustin A. Moskovitz, a 10% owner and director of Asana, purchased 450,000 shares of Class A Common Stock on July 23 and July 24, 2025, for a total amount of $6,739,155. The weighted average purchase price was $15.0427 per share on July 23 and $14.9091 per share on July 24, indicating multiple transactions at varying prices. Following these transactions, Moskovitz directly owns 54,630,999 shares and indirectly owns 4,147,046 shares through the Dustin A. Moskovitz Trust.

SEC Filing: Asana, Inc. [ ASAN ] - Form 4 - Jul. 25, 2025

data.title: BXP Inc expected to post earnings of 41 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• BXP Inc , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on July 29 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Boston Massachusetts-based company is expected to report a 0.9% increase in revenue to $857.849 million from $850.48 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 9 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for BXP Inc is for earnings of 41 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 8 "strong buy" or "buy," 13 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.0% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for BXP Inc is $77.00, about 6.8% above its last closing price of $71.80

The company's guidance on April 29 2025 for the period ended June 30 was for Funds Flow from Operations between USD1.65 and USD1.67.

Previous quarterly performance (using preferred earnings measure in US dollars). ​

data.title: H2O America expected to post earnings of 70 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• H2O America is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on July 28 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The San Jose California-based company is expected to report a 6.3% increase in revenue to $187.333 million from $176.17 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 3 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for H2O America is for earnings of 70 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for H2O America is $60.50, about 18.5% above its last closing price of $49.31

data.title: Materials Up Ahead of Fed Meeting — Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials rose, and were set to test all-time highs ahead of the Federal Reserve's meeting.

The U.S. dollar is trading near multiyear lows, giving dollar-denominated commodities a boost ahead of the Fed meeting. Gold futures fell 0.6% for the week, however, as reported progress in trade talks between the U.S. and the European Union drained safe-haven demand.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Correction to Wine Store Article on July 24 — WSJ
data.description: The 2024 Le Fraghe Ròdon Chiaretto di Bardolino is from the Veneto region of Italy. "What Makes a Great Wine Store, According to Our Columnist," at 2 p.m. ET on July 24, incorrectly said the wine is from Piedmont.

data.title: Safe Bulkers Inc expected to post earnings of 7 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Safe Bulkers Inc , is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on July 29 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Monaco Monaco-based company is expected to report a 20.4% decrease in revenue to $62.493 million from $78.55 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Safe Bulkers Inc is for earnings of 7 cents per share.

data.title: Did BellRing Brands, Inc. (BRBR) Mislead Investors? Levi & Korsinsky Investigates
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 25, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of BellRing Brands, Inc. ("BellRing Brands, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On May 6, 2025, BellRing Brands during its second quarter of 2025 earnings call revealed that certain customers were now choosing to "optimize" their inventories by lowering "their weeks of supply on hand," which would slow sales growth in the third quarter to "low-single-digits."

Following this news, the price of BellRing Brands fell by nearly 19% on the same day. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/bellring-brands-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=158246&wire=1&utm_campaign=47

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

SOURCE: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: U.S. Triples Anti-Dumping Duties on Canadian Lumber
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--The U.S. Commerce Department said Friday it agreed to nearly triple the current anti-dumping levies on Canadian softwood lumber to more than 20%.

The decision, telegraphed back in March, comes as both Prime Minister Mark Carney and senior U.S. lawmakers indicate there has been movement on a possible resolution to the decades-long bilateral trade dispute focused on Canadian lumber used to build homes.

With the ruling, lumber-industry lobbyists based in British Columbia--the province where the bulk of forest-product exports originate from--fear the total duties on Canadian softwood lumber and associated products may exceed 30%.

"These unjustified and punitive trade actions continue to harm workers, families, and communities across British Columbia and Canada-and have gone unresolved for far too long," the B.C. Council of Forest Industries said in a statement.

The B.C. Lumber Trade Council said it wanted officials in both Ottawa and Washington to find a resolution. This week, Sen. Ron Wyden, a Democrat from Oregon, traveled to Ottawa and met with Canadian Prime Minister Carney. Wyden told reporters Carney is open to resolving a long-standing row about softwood lumber by accepting a quota on how much Canadian producers can ship to the U.S.

The U.S. has long said the duties on Canadian lumber are necessary because Canadian governments were subsidizing the domestic forest-products industry, and that the Canadian sector was dumping--or selling goods into the U.S. at a lower-than-normal price--lumber into the U.S. market.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

data.title: Starbucks' Spending Hikes Are Misdirected — Market Talk
data.description:
1739 ET - Starbucks is throwing money at their stores to draw customers, but they may be losing sight of deeper issues, say Oppenheimer analysts. Starbucks is hiking labor and operational costs to improve operations and stimulate traffic, but the company's problems may instead "stem from deeper demand headwinds from competitive encroachment, over-saturation, and price/value perception," the analysts say. The increased spending will drag down the company's bottom line and make it difficult to justify the 33x forward price/earnings ratio the stock is currently trading at. Store operational expenses appear to be increasing 7% for the year, the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1722 ET - Honeywell International's long-term growth trajectory remains intact despite uncertain trends in the near term, Citi analysts say in a note. The stock's relatively weak recent performance likely reflects mixed demand shifts, with some delays in large energy projects and destocking-related uncertainty, but such headwinds should prove temporary, the analysts say. Longer term, the company's solid order activity and backlog across most end markets should support growth, making the stock's pullback an attractive buying opportunity, they say.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1624 ET - Shake Shack's 2Q results should mark another period of strong sales growth given recent efforts spanning menu innovation, marketing and promotional activity, UBS analysts say in a note. Such sustainable traffic and sales momentum is key to the stock's near- to medium-term trajectory, given high investor expectations and an over 70% gain for shares since early April, the analysts say. They raise their price target on the stock to $140 from $100 given recent sales and traffic momentum amid a tough macroeconomic backdrop. Shares rise 2.7% to close at $137.39. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1604 ET - The S&P 500 and Nasdaq set record closes again with progress in trade negotiations and the latest round of corporate earnings. President Trump is set to meet with European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen on Sunday amid signs the two sides could be closing in on a trade deal. Intel shares fall 8.5% after the chip maker says it would cut 15% of staff. Deckers Outdoor gains 11% as results beat forecasts and Boston Beer rises 6.5% after the Samuel Adams brewer projects smaller-than-previously-expected tariff headwinds. DJIA rises 208 points, or 0.5%, to 44901, the S&P 500 gains 0.4% to 6388 and the Nasdaq adds 0.2% to 21108. (patrick.sullivan@wsj.com)

1542 ET - After a quiet week for U.S. economic updates, July closes with a bang, with a head-spinning slew of headline-worthy data. Tuesday brings the first look at international goods trade in June, plus the June JOLTS report on the labor market and The Conference Board's latest survey results. Wednesday, we get the first look at official 2Q GDP, as well as the ADP private-sector jobs survey for July and a post-meeting press conference with Fed Chair Jerome Powell. In addition to the weekly jobless-claims numbers, Thursday will see an update on June personal income, spending, and the Fed's preferred gauge of consumer inflation from the Commerce Department. Friday kicks off August with the July jobs report, some PMI snapshots, the Michigan consumer poll and official construction-spending figures. (matt.grossman@wsj.com, @mattgrossman)

1531 ET - Treasury yields lose steam and end the week mixed, ahead of the Fed meeting and an increased flow of data. Interest rates are expected to remain on hold Wednesday, with much attention to be paid to any indication that a cut is forthcoming. Housing, consumer, labor and other data points are due next week. On Friday, July payrolls are expected to print 102,000 in a WSJ survey, down from June's 147,000, with unemployment ticking higher to 4.2% from 4.1%. The 10-year sheds 0.047 percentage point this week, to 4.385%, while the two-year rises 0.039 p.p. to 3.916%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1435 ET - Fixed-income strategy has become very straightforward, at least when it comes to Treasurys: buy short term and sell long term, RBC's Andrzej Skiba says. The Fed is likely to start cutting rates in the next few months, pushing up the price of short-term government debt, while long term remains under pressure as widening deficits call for increasing debt issuance. "Buy two-year and sell 30-year" Treasurys, Skiba recommends. Corporate debt is trickier because "there is no much left on the table," Skiba says. He sees pockets of opportunity in insurance and technology, for example. California utilities battered by recent disasters are also attractive, he says. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1430 ET - The Trump-Powell tug-of-war about interest rate cuts looms over the upcoming Fed meeting, but markets could be in for a treat, RBS's Andrzej Skiba says. While Powell is expected to reaffirm the Fed's independence and keep rates on hold, he may also offer an "olive branch" and crack the door open for a cut if inflation behaves as expected, Skiba says. One thing that could scramble the path to lower rates would be an escalation in trade wars stoking inflation fears. Otherwise, an economic cooldown should allow the Fed to start cutting, he says. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1346 ET - Boston Beer's line of canned iced tea-and-vodka drinks, Sun Cruiser, is "the next iconic brand for the company," CEO Michael Spillane says on a call with analysts. Time will tell, but the company has already notched four successful waves of innovation in the ready-to-drink space, first with craft beers, then ciders, followed by hard seltzers and most recently hard tea, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. Investors will definitely be wondering if it can create the magic a fifth time now with Sun Cruiser, the analysts say. Sun Cruiser has been one of the top volume gainers in the ready-to-drink spirits category so far this year, Chairman Jim Koch says on a call with analysts. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1329 ET - The amount of CBOT trading as the week finishes is very low, says AgResource in a note. "It's the dog days of summer and few traders are wanting to add to their market risk," says the firm. Weather conditions are the central focus for traders as August closes in, and without any major disruption, bumper crops are expected for both corn and soybeans. AgResource adds that the "yield potential" for both of these crops are seen as rising. CBOT corn is down 0.7%, soybeans fall 0.4%, and wheat is off 1%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1318 ET - Boston Beer took an appropriately conservative approach to its full-year guidance in the face of prolonged uncertainty about industry demand, Deutsche Bank analysts say in a research note. It is also unclear how burgeoning cost pressures related to tariffs are going to play out, justifying the brewer's cautious outlook, they say. The new view includes a wide and varied range of possibilities, likely with a better margin performance but softer revenue, the analysts say. The upper-end of the revised guidance looks achievable, so long as underlying depletion trends hold steady and don't deteriorate further, they say. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1315 ET - Boston Beer nearly reached its goal of 50% gross margin in 2Q, even as depletions softened and it absorbed tariff costs. The company's Sun Cruiser iced tea-and-vodka product is gross margin accretive and gaining steam, CEO Michael Spillane says on a call with analysts. Boston Beer has also improved brewery efficiencies, raised prices and improved its product mix, CFO Diego Reynoso says on the call. The performance is compelling in part because the 50% gross margin is clearly achievable without shifting all the way to 90% in-house production, as Boston Beer has said it wants to do, Roth analyst Bill Kirk says in a research note. The 49.8% hit in 2Q came with just 76% in-house production, the analyst says. Boston Beer climbs 6%. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

data.title: New to The Street Show #680 to Air Saturday, July 26 at 6:30 PM EST on Bloomberg TV
data.description:
Featuring DataVault, NRX Pharmaceuticals, FLOKI, Interlink Labs, and Arrive AI

Sponsored Programming by Arrive AI, MUSQ - The Music ETF, NRX Pharma, Commercialville TV, and The Sustainable Green Team

NEW YORK CITY, NEW YORK / ACCESS Newswire / July 25, 2025 / New to The Street, a premier national business television program, will broadcast Episode #680 on Saturday, July 26 at 6:30 PM EST on Bloomberg TV as sponsored programming.

This new episode features dynamic interviews and updates from five industry-disrupting companies:

  • DataVault Holdings - Revolutionizing secure data monetization using blockchain and AI technologies.

  • NRX Pharmaceuticals - Advancing cutting-edge therapies in psychiatry and infectious disease.

  • FLOKI - A leading crypto project merging meme power with real-world utility and DeFi innovation.

  • Interlink Labs - Bridging biotechnology and artificial intelligence through advanced research and development.

  • Arrive AI - Redefining last-mile delivery with intelligent, contactless logistics powered by AI and automation.

The program is proudly supported by its sponsoring partners:

Arrive AI, MUSQ - The Music ETF, NRX Pharma, Commercialville TV, and The Sustainable Green Team - each playing a vital role in the innovation economy.

About Us - New to The Street

Since 2009, New to The Street has been a trusted financial media brand, producing and broadcasting content that drives awareness, credibility, and market visibility for companies across all sectors. With national broadcasts on Fox Business and Bloomberg Television, we have built the largest linear TV audience in the business and financial news sector.

Our YouTube channel, @NewToTheStreetTV, leads the industry with over 3.1 million subscribers, offering expansive on-demand reach. This is reinforced by a social media following of over 511,000 across Facebook, LinkedIn, X (formerly Twitter), and Instagram.

We enhance every campaign with iconic outdoor media - including digital billboards in Times Square, Nasdaq and Reuters towers, and bus shelters throughout New York's Financial District - delivering unmatched brand exposure and amplification.

New to The Street is the only platform combining long-form television, sponsored and earned media, social engagement, and outdoor branding into one predictable and scalable media ecosystem.

For Media Inquiries:

Monica Brennan

Media Relations - New to The Street

Monica@NewToTheStreet.com

www.NewToTheStreet.com

YouTube.com/@NewToTheStreetTV

SOURCE: New To The Street

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Apple's Dating?App Fee Ruling Paused
data.description:
Apple gets a reprieve as the Netherlands regulator pauses its dating?app fee ruling.

The ACM said it's holding off on a final decision about the fees Apple charges dating app providers while Brussels and Cupertino hammer out their own deal.

Apple already tweaked its fee schedule and promises more adjustments later this year, so ACM wants to avoid duplicating discussions.

It'll revisit the issue once there's clarityand aims to decide by April 1, 2026. Last month the Rotterdam court backed ACM's finding that Apple abused its market dominance with unfair App Store conditions dating back to August 2021, forcing earlier concessions.

Why it matters: This pause buys Apple time to align global fee changes and could set a template for regulators worldwide.

Investors will be watching for any hints from the EU?Apple talks and ACM's ruling ahead of spring 2026.
